summary: An abstract from the EPIC-Norfolk prospective population study demonstrating that poor self-reported mental health-related quality of life is independently associated with an increased risk of all-cause mortality.

principles.statement: Poor self-reported mental functional health is an independent predictor of all-cause mortality.
principles.rationale: In the EPIC-Norfolk cohort, a 1 standard deviation decrease in mental component summary scores was associated with a 14% increase in all-cause mortality, even after controlling for physical health, lifestyle, and biological risk factors.
principles.source_quote: Poor self-reported mental functional health is related to increased risk of all-cause mortality in men and women.
